key_insight: The paper proposes a reusable multi-level ontology architecture that separates abstraction level and consumer audience, with a mechanistic seven-tier core for material-specific explanation.
engineering_relevance: It provides a scalable framework for linking materials, manufacturing, supply-chain, and lifecycle data into digital product passports while reducing ontology redesign effort across material classes.
method: Define four abstraction levels: L0 bridges, L1 material-agnostic lab-notebook, L2 material-class-specific, and L3 categorical reasoning.
Organize the L2 material layer with a seven-tier explanation skeleton: Symmetry, Energy/DFT, Thermo/CALPHAD, Kinetics, Microstructure, Defect chemistry, and Bonding.
Implement the architecture in OCO v0.94 with OWL axioms, SHACL shapes, and cross-ontology bridge mappings.
evidence_signal: The reference implementation, OCO v0.94, reports 5,196 classes, 167,348 OWL axioms, 1,674 properties, 829 bridge mappings, 1,172 SHACL shapes, and 163 competency questions.
watch_item: The ontology is large and modular, so practical adoption may depend on maintaining mapping quality and keeping the seven-tier explanation model consistent across domains.
paper_title: A Multi-Level Architecture for Reusable Materials Ontologies -- The OntoCrafter Ceramics Ontology (OCO) as Reference Implementation
